name: Cartography (Infrastructure Attack Surface Graph)

auth.notes: AWS IAM role or access keys; Neo4j credentials; GitHub/GCP credentials per integration

performance.notes: Sync time depends on account size; designed for periodic batch runs

agent_readiness.notes: Infrastructure graph tool: syncs cloud assets to Neo4j for security analysis. cartography --neo4j-uri bolt://... --aws-sync-all-profiles. Ingests: AWS (EC2, S3, IAM, RDS, Lambda), GCP, GitHub, Okta, Duo, GSuite, PagerDuty, Kubernetes. Neo4j graph queries: MATCH (u:AWSUser)-[:ASSUMES]->(r:AWSRole) RETURN u,r. Find blast radius, attack paths, permissions analysis. Custom intel modules. Used by Lyft and many enterprises for CSPM-style analysis.

gotchas: Setup complexity — multiple IAM permissions across accounts
Sync can take hours for large AWS environments
Community maintained (Lyft less active) — verify module coverage
best_when: Building infrastructure attack surface graph across cloud accounts
Security teams wanting graph-based IAM/resource analysis
Blast radius analysis: 'what does this compromised role have access to?'
Open-source CSPM alternative to commercial tools
avoid_when: When commercial CSPM (Wiz, Orca) budget available
summary: Cartography syncs cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, GitHub, Okta) into Neo4j as a graph for security analysis. MATCH (u:AWSUser)-[:ASSUMES]->(r:AWSRole) for attack path queries. Blast radius analysis, IAM permissions graphs, cross-account relationships. Open-source CSPM-style tool from Lyft. Requires Neo4j and multi-account cloud credentials. Complex setup but powerful for infrastructure security graphs.
